title: malus longipes
intro: Malus longipes, also known as the Chinese apple or wild apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of southern China. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Malus longipes include reducing fever, relieving coughs and sore throats, and aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Malus longipes include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, capsules, and topical creams for various health benefits.
